self_served.anchor_matched: All materials are, by DESIGN, safe, recognisable to children of the age range, and light enough for them to handle alone. The activity is designed for self-service even if teachers are observed helping during the recording.

evidence.quote: There are four small wooden tables with blue mats underneath them, arranged in a square. A teacher is sitting on the floor with two children, coloring. Three other children are standing around the tables, looking at papers on the floor or on the tables.
evidence.indicator: Materials are sized for child hands
evidence.reasoning: Small wooden tables sized for young children and individual colouring sheets (paper) are clearly designed for child-scale use and self-handling.

evidence.quote: Another child is walking around the room, picking up papers from the floor.
evidence.indicator: Items are light enough for a child of the age range to lift and carry alone
evidence.reasoning: A child independently picks up papers from the floor, demonstrating the materials are light enough for self-service without adult assistance.

evidence.quote: The children continue to color at the tables. The room is empty of adults.
evidence.indicator: Materials are child-safe by design (no choking hazards, sharp edges, toxic substances within reach)
evidence.reasoning: Children continue colouring independently with no adults present, confirming the materials (colouring sheets and presumably crayons/markers) are safe and manageable by design for 3–5 year olds.

evidence.quote: A teacher is sitting on the floor with two children, coloring.
evidence.indicator: Materials are recognisable to children of the age range — children would know what they are and roughly how to use them
evidence.reasoning: Colouring sheets and colouring tools are universally familiar materials for 3–5 year olds, and children are observed engaging with them independently throughout the session.

self_served.scorer_notes: The activity uses colouring sheets and presumably crayons/markers — all standard, child-familiar, lightweight, and safe materials. Children are observed handling them independently, including when no adults are present. No heavy, sharp, or unrecognisable items are visible.
